snippet: While the story is pathetic and the game presents some performance issues on switch, Bayonetta 3 takes a lesson in combat and gameplay design in a lushly stylish sequence.
title: Bayonetta 3 - Review
Alan Wake II: Night Springs DLC Review
snippet: Alan Wake II: Night Springs veers from ultra violence to tense survival horror, before settling into the multiversal madness of the series at its best.

title: F1 24 Review
snippet: In isolation, F1 24 remains a slick, deep, and marvellous motorsports experience, but it’s hard to argue it’s essential for returning players.

title: 1000xResist Review
snippet: 1000xResist is a revolution in storytelling, creating its own complex and rich fiction that’s beautifully written and spectacularly visualized. By expertly weaving genuine intimate moments with a sharp political identity, it makes its statements with a confidence that leaves an everlasting impact.

game.name: Killer Klowns from Outer Space: The Game

title: Killer Klowns from Outer Space: The Game Review
snippet: Clownin’ around in Killer Klowns From Outer Space: The Game is hilarious and action-packed, but runs its course rather quickly.

title: Capes Review
snippet: Capes is a clever and challenging tactics game with a clear love of the superhero comics that inspired it. And no one actually wears a cape.

title: XDefiant Review
snippet: The fundamentals of XDefiant are good, but conflicting ideas and mechanics stop it from standing above a crowded shooter field.

title: Senua's Saga: Hellblade II Review
snippet: Senua’s Saga: Hellblade II is another Viking-worthy feast for the senses that meets the high bar set by its predecessor, even if it never really manages to clear it.

title: Paper Mario: The Thousand-Year Door Review
snippet: Paper Mario: The Thousand-Year Door is an amazingly loyal and visually dazzling remake of a treasured RPG, and the improvements made throughout easily make this the definitive way to experience Mario’s unforgettable quest.

title: Homeworld 3 Single-Player Campaign Review
snippet: Homeworld 3's single-player campaign brings the series' epic space battles in for intense, close-quarters combat on visually diverse maps full of enormous obstacles to plan around. At the same time, it delivers an unexpectedly personal story that leans a little too heavily on the cliche of a new-generation protagonist seeking out the old.

title: Homeworld 3 Multiplayer Review
snippet: Homeworld 3's multiplayer skirmish mode may be barebones, but it gives us all we need to wage visually impressive war with fleets of starships. The co-op War Games mode, on the other hand, gets an extra boost from its novel roguelite-style progression.

title: Animal Well Review
snippet: Animal Well is a beautiful, multi-layered puzzle box that’s both fun to simply play around with, and an utter delight to slowly crack open, secret by secret.

title: Little Kitty, Big City Review
snippet: Little Kitty, Big City does not reach for the stars, but it is a fun little romp for anyone looking for a relaxed adventure.

title: Fallout 76 Review - 2024
snippet: In 2024, Fallout 76 finally captures a lot of the post-nuclear experience I love. It trades roleplaying decision-making for multiplayer shooter antics, but it still needs more endgame content and a fair inventory solution.

title: Stardew Valley Review - 2024
snippet: More than just a cozy farming sim, eight years of updates have grown Stardew Valley into a modern classic with an endless list of enticing activities to complete and a deeply satisfying time management challenge to take on.

title: Hades 2 Early Access Review
snippet: Even in its Early Access state, Hades 2 is just about everything one could ask for in a sequel to one of the best roguelites of all time. Featuring excellent refinements to its roguelite progression, a fantastic new main character in Melinoe, and two unique sets of levels that have you fighting foes on the surface and in the underworld.

title: Homeworld: Vast Reaches Review
snippet: Homeworld: Vast Reaches gets the atmosphere of this classic strategy series right, but oversimplifies and speeds up its space battles to the point where it loses the feeling of being in command of a fleet – all without doing anything that couldn't have been accomplished outside of VR.

title: Endless Ocean Luminous Review
snippet: Boring in multiplayer and frustrating alone, Endless Ocean Luminous is a tedious, aggravating slog that feels endless in all the wrong ways.

title: Another Crab’s Treasure Review
snippet: Another Crab’s Treasure throws out dark themes and gratuitous violence in favor of talking cartoon crabs, and I love it.

title: Sand Land Review
snippet: Sand Land’s vehicular focus shows potential, but devolves into a by-the-numbers open world action game with storytelling that only occasionally manages to live up to the joys of the original work.

title: Stellar Blade Review
snippet: Stellar Blade is great in all of the most important ways for an action game, but dull characters, a lackluster story, and several frustrating elements of its RPG mechanics prevent it from soaring along with the best of the genre.

title: TopSpin 2K25 Review
snippet: TopSpin 2K25 is the most enjoyable way to get a realistic rally going in your own home without building an actual court in your backyard.

title: Manor Lords Early Access Review
snippet: Manor Lords is a gorgeous medieval city-builder that kept me occupied for hours building my perfect, cozy hamlets, but it definitely feels very early in its Early Access development.

game.name: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Arcade: Wrath of the Mutants

title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Arcade: Wrath of the Mutants Review
snippet: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Arcade: Wrath of the Mutants is like a two-dollar slice of pizza you pick up while you’re walking home after a night out in New York City. It’s tasty in the moment, but it will leave you wanting a lot more.

title: Tales of Kenzera: Zau Review
snippet: Tales of Kenzera: Zau's action isn't groundbreaking within the genre, but its elevated by a truly moving tale about how to go on in this world when your loved ones have passed on to the next.

title: No Rest for the Wicked Early Access Review
snippet: No Rest for the Wicked is a compelling and unique action-RPG with a lot going for it, and lots of room still to grow.

title: Eiyuden Chronicle: Hundred Heroes Review
snippet: One you get past its slow start, Eiyuden Chronicle: Hundred Heroes mostly succeeds in weaving the stories of multiple countries and characters together into a sprawling epic.

title: Harold Halibut Review
snippet: It may take place at the bottom of the ocean on a distant alien planet, but Harold Halibut’s story is an entirely human one, both in terms of its carefully handmade environments and its strong emphasis on empathy towards its eccentric cast of characters. While I wish that there was a little more interactivity to be found throughout its subaquatic setting, and Harold’s shuffling run could have been boosted to a sprint to reduce the time spent repeatedly schlepping from one end of its levels to the other, I nonetheless remained captivated by his whimsical quest to unite his community and explore the spectacular surroundings of his sunken home. Consistently funny and full of surprises, Harold Halibut is a wholesome, handcrafted adventure with plenty of heart.

title: Broken Roads Review
snippet: A disappointing RPG with interesting but poorly executed ideas, Broken Roads lives up to its name in all the wrong ways.

title: Children of the Sun Review
snippet: This slick and shrewd supernatural sniping game is packed with satisfying spatial problems to solve (and shoot), but it's not significantly satisfying after the first time around.

title: Botany Manor Review
snippet: Clever clues entwine with Botany Manor’s charming old English setting to make for a lighthearted first-person puzzler worth tending to.

title: Astra: Knights of Veda Review
snippet: Astra: Knights of Veda’s detailed world and challenging combat show promise, but its overly aggressive grind and sluggish progression dampen the charm.

title: Pepper Grinder Review
snippet: A short but sweet slice of propulsive platforming action, Pepper Grinder is all driller and no filler.

title: Open Roads Review
snippet: An earnest exploration of a family in turmoil, Open Roads has sharp teeth but ultimately lacks bite.

title: Outpost: Infinity Siege Review
snippet: An incredibly unique mix of FPS, RTS, and tower defense ideas, Outpost: Infinity Siege is absurdly complicated but a whole lot of fun.

title: South Park: Snow Day! Review
snippet: Aggravating hack-and-slash combat and surprisingly sparse jokes make South Park: Snow Day! dull, toothless, and a big step in the wrong direction for South Park games.

title: Millennia Review
snippet: Millennia focuses too much on the ways it wants to mix up the Civilization formula without building the base of a strong Civ-like first.

title: Princess Peach: Showtime! Review
snippet: Princess Peach: Showtime! is a simple, cheery romp with a variety of neat styles and a fun theater-inspired setting.

title: Rise of the Ronin Review
snippet: Rise of the Ronin marries a cool setting, Team Ninja’s mastery of tough-but-fair combat, and smart RPG design, even when map clutter and junk loot can get in its way.

title: Dragon’s Dogma 2 Review
snippet: More of a redo than a sequel, Dragon's Dogma 2 is a strange and wonderful action-RPG that bolsters the original’s strengths without addressing its weaknesses.

title: MLB The Show 24 Review
snippet: MLB The Show 24 continues to push the genre forward – not every choice is a homerun, but it has enough hits for another all-star appearance.

title: Alone in the Dark Review
snippet: It’s not without its memorable moments, but Alone in the Dark fails to escape the shadows of the other contemporary survival horror titans that it helped spawn.

title: Lightyear Frontier Early Access Review
snippet: Cute and chill, homesteading in your giant mech on Lightyear Frontier’s alien world is already some good and simple fun, even if it currently stops a bit short.

title: Tribes 3: Rivals Early Access Review
snippet: Tribes 3: Rivals is a rocket-powered sequel that packs some serious horsepower, but its current Early Access options run out of fuel quite quickly.

title: Backpack Battles Early Access Review
snippet: Backpack Battles’ satisfying inventory organizing mechanics make for an autobattler with a strong foundation, but its barebones Early Access launch leaves a lot of its potential waiting on the table.

title: Outcast: A New Beginning Review
snippet: Outcast: A New Beginning is an effective restart for this all-but-forgotten series. It's certainly rough around the edges, but outside of Just Cause you probably won't have more fun with movement in an open-world game than you will here.

title: Contra: Operation Galuga Review
snippet: Contra: Operation Galuga is an amusing run-and-gun that met my 2D shooting expectations, but rarely exceeded them.

title: Balatro Review
snippet: A deck-building poker roguelike of endlessly satisfying proportions, Balatro is the sort of fun that threatens to derail whole weekend plans.

title: Nightingale Early Access Review
snippet: Nightingale has a cool setting and some fun ideas at its Early Access launch, but right now its compelling survival-crafting progression is bogged down by frustrating pacing and an underserved story.

title: Unicorn Overlord Review
snippet: Unicorn Overlord is a visual delight that's brimming with creativity, and an absolute must-play for any fan of strategy RPGs.

title: Expeditions: A MudRunner Game Review
snippet: Expeditions: A MudRunner Game isn’t a replacement for the supremely addictive SnowRunner, but its more untamed wilderness maps make it a worthwhile complementary experience.

title: The Thaumaturge Review
snippet: The Thaumaturge is a slow, text heavy caper that is often curious and sometimes captivating, weaving revolutionary labor politics and mysticism together to create a unique and charming piece of historical science fiction.

title: The Outlast Trials Review
snippet: The Outlast Trials is a bloody cooperative horror game that burns brightly, but fizzles after a few enjoyable hours.

title: WWE 2K24 Review
snippet: While not drastically different, WWE 2K24 is better in almost every way, touting small but smart additions to well-tested systems and modes as opposed to taking bigger risks.

title: Terminator: Dark Fate - Defiance Review
snippet: Terminator: Dark Fate - Defiance may look the part, but the obnoxious mission design and confused campaign structure of this RTS just aren't worth your time.

title: Splatoon 3: Side Order DLC Review
snippet: A few fishy elements aside, Splatoon 3’s Side Order DLC is a delectable roguelite expansion with outstanding original enemies and crazy new abilities.

title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth Review
snippet: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth impressively builds off of what Remake set in motion, both as a best-in-class action-RPG full of exciting challenges and an awe-inspiring recreation of a world that has meant so much to so many for so long.

title: Last Epoch Review
snippet: Push past its one-note campaign and Last Epoch is an impressive, time-bending action RPG that combines rich customization with modern action.

title: Pacific Drive Review
snippet: Balancing Pacific Drive’s scrappy systems can be punishing, but the riveting tension of each drive through its unnerving world was always enough to keep my hands placed firmly on the wheel.

title: Foamstars Review
snippet: Foamstars' combat mechanics are unexpectedly engaging, but confounding time-gated modes and aggressive monetization make them harder to enjoy.

title: Skull and Bones Review
snippet: Skull and Bones is a maritime RPG with a strong foundation, even if it feels like a live-service first draft.

title: Helldivers 2 Review
snippet: Helldivers 2's combat feels fantastic, its missions stay fresh and interesting, and its smart progression system doesn’t nickel and dime you.

title: Mario vs. Donkey Kong Review
snippet: Mario vs. Donkey Kong might not be as polished as a Mini-Mario toy fresh off the assembly line, but it's just as full of charm and fun to play with.

title: Atomic Heart: Trapped in Limbo Review
snippet: A disappointing mix of ideas that ends as an exhausting Temple Run clone, Atomic Heart: Trapped in Limbo gets less fun the longer it goes goes on.

title: Banishers: Ghosts of New Eden Review
snippet: Banishers: Ghosts of New Eden’s memorable story and inspired investigative cases help carry it across its rougher patches.

title: The Inquisitor Review
snippet: Playing a medieval church cop hunting a vampire is a great concept, but blurry choices, weak combat, and a ton of handholding keep The Inquisitor from leading a compelling inquisition.

game.name: Suicide Squad: Kill The Justice League

title: Suicide Squad: Kill the Justice League Review
snippet: Suicide Squad: Kill the Justice League is a repetitive and bland looter-shooter that, despite an engaging story, never stays fun for long enough.

title: Granblue Fantasy: Relink Review
snippet: Granblue Fantasy: Relink has a succinct story, fantastic battle system, and fun multiplayer options that help it stand out.

title: Persona 3 Reload Review
snippet: With a stellar visual overhaul and countless small but impactful changes, Persona 3 Reload tells a timeless story of tragedy and hope with sharp emotional sincerity.

title: Enshrouded Early Access Review
snippet: Enshrouded’s mix of Valheim-style survival and Breath of the Wild-style exploration make a wonderful pairing, but it still has slew of Early Access kinks to work out.

title: Palworld Early Access Review - Xbox/PC Game Pass Version
snippet: The version of Palworld on Xbox and the Microsoft Store might not be nearly as polished as the Steam version right now, but that doesn’t mean it isn’t still a ton of fun.

title: Stargate: Timekeepers Review
snippet: Stargate: Timekeepers is a challenging and enjoyable stealth tactics game that lovingly pays homage to the classic television series that inspired it.

title: Tekken 8 Review
snippet: Tekken 8 is an incredible evolution for the series, with tons of single player content, an excellent suite of training tools, a great online experience, and exciting new mechanics that make Tekken more dynamic than ever.

title: Like a Dragon: Infinite Wealth Review
snippet: Sprawling, enthralling, and packed with dynamic brawling, Like a Dragon: Infinite Wealth isn’t just the best turn-based Like a Dragon game, it’s one of the greatest games in the entire series.

title: Palworld Early Access Review - Steam Version
snippet: Palworld may crib quite a bit from Pokémon’s homework, but deep survival mechanics and a hilarious attitude make it hard to put down – even in Early Access.

title: Another Code: Recollection Review
snippet: Another Code: Recollection is a chill, breezy return for a dormant Nintendo series that’s light on challenge but heavy on charm.

title: Turnip Boy Robs a Bank Review
snippet: Turnip Boy Robs a Bank might have changed genres, but big improvements to combat and a more jam-packed world revitalize the series.

game.name: Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown

title: Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown Review
snippet: Returning to a 2D perspective lets Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown perfect its controls and combat while capturing everything that was great about the heyday of the series.

title: Supernormal Review
snippet: Despite an uneven start and a woefully predictable story, Supernormal can offer some sincerely spooky scares.

title: Fortnite Rocket Racing Review
snippet: Fortnite Rocket Racing is certainly fun for a few rounds, but there's not enough here yet to stay fresh very long.

game.name: Pokémon Scarlet & Violet: The Indigo Disk

title: Pokemon Scarlet and Violet: The Indigo Disk DLC Review
snippet: The Indigo Disk is a blast to play alone and with friends, but it’s still held back by all the same technical issues that made Pokemon Scarlet and Violet a disaster.

title: Fortnite Festival Review
snippet: Fortnite Festival’s limited, isolating gameplay and overpriced tracks may turn Fortnite players into rhythm game fans, but it won’t turn rhythm game fans into Fortnite players.

title: The Finals Review
snippet: The Finals is a fresh and exciting take on team based PVP shooters, featuring some of the best environmental destruction in any FPS.

title: Granblue Fantasy Versus: Rising Review
snippet: Granblue Fantasy Versus Rising fully realizes the potential that the vanilla game showed back in 2020. This is a feature rich and absolutely gorgeous 2D anime fighter with a deep roster of 28 fantastic characters, great netcode, and well implemented gameplay tweaks that reinvigorate the already exciting fighting system.

title: Cookie Cutter Review
snippet: Despite some occasional frustrations, Cookie Cutter is a gory, gorgeous, and bloody good Metroidvania.

title: House Flipper 2 Review
snippet: House Flipper 2 lets you live your HGTV dreams with a renovation sim that turns window cleaning and wallpapering into satisfying adventures.

title: Warhammer 40,000: Rogue Trader Review
snippet: Warhammer 40,000: Rogue Trader is an ultra crunchy, 130-hour space epic with excellent writing and combat, but unfortunately, also a lot of bugs.

title: Resident Evil 4 Remake VR Review
snippet: Resident Evil 4 Remake VR Mode is just about as great as you’d expect, but the base game’s third-person perspective made Capcom’s job a lot harder.

title: Asgard’s Wrath 2 Review
snippet: Asgard’s Wrath 2 is an open-world action RPG that sets a new gold standard for VR – and competes with the best anywhere.

title: Lego Fortnite Review
snippet: Lego Fortnite has an approachable, chill vibe thanks to its helpful NPCs, but right now its empty-feeling world seems like a foundation waiting to be built upon.

title: The Day Before Early Access Review
snippet: The Day Before is easily one of the worst games I’ve ever played, to the point where I’m afraid to continue running it on my PC – and if you didn’t manage to try it, you can count yourself as one of the lucky ones.

title: The Anacrusis Review
snippet: The Anacrusis is a co-op shooter with remarkably few surprises and surprisingly unremarkable gunplay.

title: Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora Review
snippet: Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora features a stunning alien world to explore, but doesn’t contain as many genuine surprises as other modern open-worlds.

title: Arizona Sunshine 2 Review
snippet: Arizona Sunshine 2 keeps the pressure on for a long campaign of zombie slaying full of satisfyingly gory head shots, entertaining humor, and the bond between a lonely man and his dog.

title: SteamWorld Build Review
snippet: SteamWorld Build is a simple but fun city builder cleverly combined with a dungeon-constructing miner that keeps things quick and casual.

title: The Walking Dead: Destinies Review
snippet: The Walking Dead: Destinies is an insult not only to the TV show it poorly attempts to emulate, but to modern gaming in general.

title: Bluey: The Videogame Review
snippet: Bluey: The Videogame may look the part but, with shonky controls and barely two hours of gameplay, everywhere else it’s a dog’s breakfast.

title: Flashback 2 Review
snippet: Flashback 2 is not a blast from the past – it’s a total misfire.

game.name: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare III

title: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 3 Zombies Review
snippet: Operation Deadlock mixes Zombies with Warzone's DMZ mode, and while the combination can create some heart-stopping battles, you have to be willing to grind to see them.

game.name: Naruto X Boruto Ultimate Ninja Storm Connections

title: Naruto x Boruto Ultimate Ninja Storm Connections Review
snippet: Naruto x Boruto Ultimate Ninja Storm Connections may have the flashiest graphics in the series, but there’s almost nothing else here to make me seek it out over past entries with basically the same fighting system and better single-player stories.

title: Assassin's Creed Nexus VR Review
snippet: Assassin’s Creed Nexus is an impressively complete Ubisoft game, even if not all those parts stick the landing in VR.

title: Super Mario RPG Review
snippet: Super Mario RPG is considered a classic for a reason, and this faithful remake makes it easy for anyone who missed it in the SNES era to see why.

title: The Last Faith Review
snippet: The Last Faith is not the pinnacle of 2D metroidvanias, but its collection of weapons is genuinely cool and busting the masters of its samey levels is satisfying.

game.name: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare III

title: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 3 Multiplayer Review
snippet: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 3 reheats and reserves the same multiplayer from Modern Warfare 2 with some extra maps and tweaks as garnishes. This smooth combat remains gaming comfort food, but slower unlock progression makes it harder to get excited about.

title: Persona 5 Tactica Review
snippet: Persona 5 Tactica is a big bag of bite-sized Phantom Thieves adventures that keeps the spirit of the series alive and shows that it can work great with a grid-based battle system.

title: For the King 2 Review
snippet: Simple and occasionally even elegant mechanics make this cooperative tabletop-style RPG fun once you get into it, but a lacking interface makes it more difficult to enjoy these straightforward adventures than it should be.

title: My Time at Sandrock Review
snippet: Whether collecting resources, building items, or passing the day away playing minigames with townies, My Time at Sandrock is a joy.

title: EA Sports WRC Review
snippet: EA Sports WRC feels like a great racing game trying to fight its way out of an unfinished one.

title: Nickelodeon All-Star Brawl 2 Review
snippet: Nickelodeon All-Star Brawl 2 knocks nearly every single one of the first game’s problems into the blast zone while also introducing the exciting new Slime mechanic, a more interesting roster, and better visuals throughout.

game.name: Like a Dragon Gaiden: The Man Who Erased His Name

title: Like a Dragon Gaiden: The Man Who Erased His Name Review
snippet: Like a Dragon Gaiden’s combat hits with flash and fury and its story is sharp, but its setting and side activities feel a bit on the stale side.

game.name: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare III

title: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 3 Single-Player Campaign Review
snippet: Underbaked, rehashed, and cobbled together from multiplayer parts, Modern Warfare 3’s single-player campaign is everything a Call of Duty story mode shouldn’t be.

title: The Talos Principle 2  Review
snippet: Despite crashes and some issues with its difficulty curve, The Talos Principle 2's ability to explore both interesting puzzle design and deep philosophy simultaneously is incredibly impressive.

title: The Invincible Review
snippet: The Invincible might have a strong inspiration in the 1964 novel, but every time it attempts to make it more interesting as an interactive experience it ends up doing the opposite.

title: Thirsty Suitors Review
snippet: The story can feel a bit rushed at times, but cooking under the pressure of a judgmental family and reconciling with exes through exaggerated battles makes Thirsty Suitors very worthwhile.

title: WarioWare: Move It! Review
snippet: WarioWare: Move It! is another amusing entry in the series, with creative multiplayer modes making up for a roster of minigames that quickly become repetitive.

title: RoboCop: Rogue City Review
snippet: RoboCop: Rogue City is a pitch-perfect throwback to the action movies of the ‘80s. It’s over-the-top violence with charm, largely well put together but rough on the edges. Most importantly, it's a fun way to spend time in a beloved fictional universe that doesn’t overstay its welcome. Blasting at goons as an unstoppable walking machine remains as extremely entertaining as it seemed on the big screen, thanks in part to an impressive commitment to capturing the look and feel of the original film. Mixing in elements like routine police work and side quests does a great job changing the pace, too. Even if it’s not the best example of visual fidelity, and prone to some bugs along the way, that love of RoboCop shines through. This is a solid B movie of a video game, which is exactly what the source material demands.

title: Quantum Error Review
snippet: Though heavy on style and creepy vibes, the scariest things going on in Quantum Error are its tepid story and characters, numerous bugs, oppressive checkpoints, and a roster of truly dumb enemies.

title: Dead Island 2: Haus DLC Review
snippet: This very brief DLC offers some quality laughs, but few other reasons to revisit Hell-A.

title: EA Sports UFC 5 Review
snippet: EA Sports UFC 5 deftly combines elements from sports simulation and fighting games into the best overall MMA game yet.

title: Alan Wake II Review
snippet: Alan Wake II is a superb survival-horror sequel that makes the cult-classic original seem like little more than a rough first draft by comparison.

game.name: The Lord of the Rings: Return to Moria

title: The Lord of the Rings: Return to Moria Review
snippet: The Lord of the Rings: Return to Moria doesn’t give a ton of reasons to play it over its genre peers – and its poor combat, building, and mining mechanics make those other options sound even more appealing.

title: Stray Souls Review
snippet: Despite a genuinely unsettling introductory chapter, Stray Souls is an ambitious but unsatisfying horror game that never quite gets going. Its characters are shallow and uninteresting, and while the sights and sounds of its world can be impressive, the mystery that unravels within it is told mostly through boring exposition dumps and left unsatisfyingly dangling. With poor optimization, zero polish, and some of the dullest bosses I've ever had the displeasure of fighting, Stray Souls is its own worst enemy, destroying its own subtlety with inelegant scares and tedious combat.

title: Ghostrunner 2 Review
snippet: Ghostrunner 2 is a sequel that builds upon everything that made the first game great, leading to 10-12 hours of absolutely action packed, high speed, cyberninja excellence, along with a great, replayable, roguelike mode as well.

title: Cities: Skylines 2 Review
snippet: Cities: Skylines 2 is an ambitious sequel that might have bitten off more than it can chew – be prepared to do a lot of terraforming if you don't want your metropolis to look like a nightmare.

title: Laika: Aged Through Blood Review
snippet: Laika: Aged Through Blood mixes motorbikes and gunplay to create an incredibly unique metroidvania experience. Bolstered by a beautiful soundtrack and brutal story, its unapologetic approach to mastering both bike and bullet rewards learning from frequents death with some seriously slick and satisfying combat.

title: Super Mario Bros. Wonder Review
snippet: Super Mario Bros. Wonder looks and plays like the true next step for 2D Mario platformers. Wonder effects change each stage in both surprising and delightful ways, the Flower Kingdom makes for a vibrant and refreshing change of pace, and Elephant Mario steals the show.

title: Hellboy: Web of Wyrd Review
snippet: Missed opportunity haunts every inch of Hellboy: Web of Wyrd. It's simple to dive into and play but all too soon you reach the bottom of its shallow gameplay. Its roguelike runs are short and sweet, but its enemies aren't a challenge worthy of Hellboy.

title: Skull Island: Rise of Kong Review
snippet: Skull Island: Rise of Kong is a boring, buggy, totally unambitious game that isn't even interesting in its failures.

title: Wizard with a Gun Review
snippet: Wizard with a Gun is an entertaining co-op action game, but balance issues and an unsatisfying ending leave it feeling unfinished and in need of updates.

title: Hot Wheels Unleashed 2: Turbocharged Review
snippet: Hot Wheels Unleashed 2 is an impeccably detailed and blisteringly fast racer with incredible graphics and rock-solid arcade underpinnings, although it’s guilty of forgetting to suitably cater to its wide audience at times.

title: Marvel's Spider-Man 2 Review
snippet: Marvel's Spider-Man 2 delivers Insomniac's best tale yet, and despite its open world falling short, is a reliably fun superhero power trip.

title: Endless Dungeon Review
snippet: Endless Dungeon is simultaneously a great roguelite, a great twin-stick shooter, and a great tower defense game.

title: Sonic Superstars Review
snippet: Sonic Superstars has a mix of both interesting and ill-advised new ideas, making it an enjoyable Sonic game but not exactly a Super one.

title: Lords of the Fallen Review
snippet: Lords of the Fallen is an awesome soulslike with a fantastic dual-realities premise, even when performance shortcomings and wimpy bosses crash the party.

title: Star Trek: Infinite Review
snippet: Star Trek: Infinite does a great job of capturing the look and feel of Star Trek, but those enticing bones splinter under the weight of its bugs and glitches.

title: NHL 24 Review
snippet: NHL 24’s new gameplay features reinvigorate the on-ice action, but its collection of unimproved game modes has grown increasingly boring with each passing year.

title: Total War: Pharaoh Review
snippet: Total War: Pharaoh's interesting battles complement an impressively rich campaign centered on the Late Bronze Age Collapse.

title: Cricket 24 Review
snippet: Cricket 24 is a disappointingly modest and often slipshod update that seemingly lacks the confidence or creativity to advance further down the wicket.

title: Forza Motorsport Review
snippet: Forza Motorsport is brimming with new features across the board, from its muscular new multiplayer to its much-improved handling, but its new RPG-inspired upgrade system feels like a step down.

title: Assassin's Creed Mirage Review
snippet: Assassin’s Creed Mirage's back-to-basics approach is a successful first step in returning to the stealthy style that launched this series.

title: Detective Pikachu Returns Review
snippet: Detective Pikachu Returns is yet another mystery whodunit in the Pokemon universe, but it’s tough to deduce who this is for, despite having all the clues.

title: EA Sports FC 24 for Switch Review
snippet: While it isn’t another literal copy-and-paste of last year’s version, EA Sports FC 24 is still leagues worse on Nintendo Switch.

title: Station to Station Review
snippet: Station to Station is a relaxing, cozy railway planner with clear goals, flexibility in how you accomplish them, and the absolute perfect amount of challenge. It doesn't try to be anything more than a fun and easy going experience, and the fact you can randomize levels and crank their difficulty up a bit provides some nice variety to how you approach each session. I love its voxel-based graphics so much, the music is soothing, and it just oozes charm. Even failing a level didn’t frustrate, because it just meant I got another opportunity to roll through the blocky countryside and do a little better. Its biggest strength lies in its simplicity, and it doesn't stray too far from that initial gameplay premise. Honestly, it doesn't have to.

title: High On Life: High on Knife DLC Review
snippet: High on Knife is a decent add-on for those searching for an excuse to jump back into Squanch Games’ gross world of naughty words and cartoon violence, but it doesn’t offer a whole lot of reasons to stick around for more than a couple of hours. With a disappointingly short questline and an ill-advised focus on some of the weaker members of the High on Life cast, it doesn’t come close to reaching the same highs (pun intended) as it did last year. There are certainly some decent laughs to be had, and more of this shamelessly idiotic world is by no means a bad thing, but there just isn’t enough here for me to be able to enthusiastically recommend anyone clear even this small amount of time on their busy gaming schedule.

title: Cocoon Review
snippet: Cocoon is one to remember. This puzzle adventure builds on ideas from Limbo and Inside and creates a mind-bending, world-warping adventure unlike either of them.

title: The Lamplighters League Review
snippet: The Lamplighters League starts slow, but its mix of squad tactics, stealth, and pulp adventure soon builds momentum thanks to excellent design of both heroes and enemies.

game.name: Avatar: The Last Airbender - Quest for Balance

title: Avatar: The Last Airbender - Quest for Balance Review
snippet: Avatar: The Last Airbender - Quest for Balance is a mess, with clunky combat, way too many pointless puzzles, and baffling choices for which scenes from the series to highlight.

title: EA Sports FC 24 Review
snippet: It might have a new name, but EA Sports FC 24 is just about the same, frustrating but beautiful game that FIFA's been for many years.

title: Wargroove 2 Review
snippet: Wargroove 2 is still one of the best tributes to Advance Wars out there, and bigger than its predecessor in every way, even though otherwise not much has changed.

title: Super Bomberman R 2 Review
snippet: Super Bomberman R 2 is a clear improvement over its predecessor and chock-full of exciting multiplayer action, even if its story mode leaves much to be desired

title: Mineko's Night Market Review
snippet: Mineko’s Night Market has lovely art, but its boring chores and lifeless NPCs make it much harder to get caught up in its rhythm than it is in other life sims.

title: The Making of Karateka Review
snippet: The Making of Karateka is part game collection and part documentary; a playable history of an 80s game Karateka that tells a remarkable true story in the coolest way possible.

title: Resident Evil 4: Separate Ways DLC Review
snippet: Separate Ways is a worthy counterpart to Resident Evil 4's fantastic story, providing an excellent remix of both new and familiar locations, and its breakneck pace of pitting you against excellent adversaries never leaves room for a dull moment.

title: Cyberpunk 2077: Phantom Liberty Review
snippet: Cyberpunk 2077: Phantom Liberty completes an immense turnaround for CD Projekt Red's future RPG kickstarted with the anime spinoff, Cyberpunk: Edgerunners and its latest 2.0 Update.

game.name: Pokemon Scarlet and Violet: The Teal Mask

title: Pokemon Scarlet and Violet: The Teal Mask DLC Review
snippet: The Teal Mask is yet another step in a slow quality downslide for this series: it runs terribly, looks ugly, feels predictable, doesn’t offer any of the freedom Scarlet and Violet’s main story did, and falls short in so many ways compared to just about every other Pokemon game and DLC I’ve played.

title: Payday 3 Review
snippet: Payday 3's cooperative heists are off to a strong start, even if the vault is a bit bare at the moment.

title: Mortal Kombat 1 For Switch Review
snippet: Mortal Kombat 1 proves to be too much for the Switch's dated hardware. The load times are egregious, there are numerous bugs plaguing both graphics and gameplay, making for a poor quality port of a great game that's all around aggravating to play.

title: Dune: Spice Wars Review
snippet: Dune: Spice Wars is a clever, multilayered, challenging RTS that skillfully translates so much of what is cool about Frank Herbert's universe in its interesting mechanics.

title: The Crew Motorfest Review
snippet: The Crew Motorfest is a robust racer with a confident sense of style, but its smaller map lacks life, its multiplayer isn't really worth the wait, and its omnipresent microtransaction opportunities are still tedious.

title: Party Animals Review
snippet: Party Animals is a chaotic, goofy, fun party game that occasionally stumbles over its inflexible rules and local multiplayer mishaps.

title: Firewall Ultra Review
snippet: A competent sequel to the multiplayer VR shooter original in most ways, Firewall Ultra's awkward use of eye tracking and lack of content are a shot in the foot rather than the arm.

title: Lies of P Review
snippet: Lies of P might not branch out particularly far from its soulslike inspiration, but it plays the part extremely well.

title: Mortal Kombat 1 Review in Progress
snippet: So far, the Kameo system and smart changes to the fighting make MK1 feel fresh and exciting, the story mode is predictably great, even if Invasions seems like more of a grind than I'm willing to put myself through to unlock the best cosmetic items.

title: NBA 2K24 Review
snippet: NBA 2K24 scores big with gameplay, but its microtransaction tactics feel like a costly turnover.
Starfield has a lot of forces working against it, but eventually the allure of its expansive roleplaying quests and respectable combat make its gravitational pull difficult to resist.

title: Trine 5: A Clockwork Conspiracy Review
snippet: Trine 5: A Clockwork Conspiracy sequel is a superb puzzle platformer that sticks close to the series’ playbook.

title: Sea of Stars Review
snippet: Sea of Stars is an excellent tribute RPG that channels the best parts of its ‘90s-era forebears. It's not wholly original, but there are enough twists in its story to keep it interesting after the combat loses its luster.

title: Madden NFL 24 Review
snippet: New animations and improved AI make Madden NFL 24’s on-field action the best it's ever been, but everything that happens off the field is a slog of dated modes and laggy menus that brings everything around it down.

game.name: Armored Core VI: Fires of Rubicon

title: Armored Core 6: Fires of Rubicon Review
snippet: Armored Core 6: Fires of Rubicon’s stellar customization options feed into its excellent mecha combat, and the result is challenging combat puzzles that kept my attention all the way through its 15-hour campaign and beyond. It’s let down by a dull story, but lands direct hits where it counts.

title: Immortals of Aveum Review
snippet: Immortals of Aveum is an impressively confident first-person shooter with a hearty solo campaign and fast-paced, spellcasting combat that’s spectacular to watch.

title: Baldur's Gate 3 Review
snippet: With crunchy, tactical RPG combat, a memorable story with complex characters, highly polished cinematic presentation, and a world that always rewards exploration and creativity, Baldur's Gate 3 is the new high-water mark for CRPGs.

title: Blasphemous 2 Review
snippet: Blasphemous 2 is an orthodox Metroidvania with style, creepy bosses, and a gloriously gross return to Cvstodia.

title: WrestleQuest Review
snippet: WrestleQuest’s love for wrestling catapults its creative and beautiful landscapes and strong characters into the main event, but isn’t enough to elevate its bloated level design or competent-but-repetitive combat out of the mid card.

title: Shadow Gambit: The Cursed Crew Review
snippet: Shadow Gambit: The Cursed Crew is dead-on stealth tactics-filled with character and vigor – a peak for the genre.

title: Gord Review
snippet: Gord is, in its most outstanding moments, a mediocre colony sim/RTS/RPG hybrid. The rest is just boring.

title: The Texas Chain Saw Massacre: The Game Review
snippet: The Texas Chain Saw Massacre's less asymmetrical take on the asymmetrical horror genre offers a few entertaining, technically challenged hours of fun.

title: Atomic Heart: Annihilation Instinct Review
snippet: Atomic Heart: Annihilation Instinct still looks the goods, but its linear approach and weirdly limited arsenal is a step down from where we left off.

title: Moving Out 2 Review
snippet: Moving Out 2 is a colourful and chaotic co-op sequel that tasks you with bending at the knees while consistently splitting your sides.

title: Atlas Fallen Review
snippet: Atlas Fallen is a solid open-world action RPG with plenty of platforming and large monsters to fight with a co-op buddy, so long as neither of you cares about story or is a stickler for high-quality textures.

title: Stray Gods: The Roleplaying Musical Review
snippet: Stray Gods: The Roleplaying Musical's murder mystery is predictable, but worth playing through a couple of times for its strong voice cast and customizable songs.

title: The Expanse: A Telltale Series Episodes 1-3 Review
snippet: A solid addition to the Telltale roster that accurately captures its source material while falling prey to the faults of the studio's other licensed games.

game.name: Double Dragon Gaiden: Rise of the Dragons

title: Double Dragon Gaiden: Rise of the Dragons Review
snippet: The breath of Double Dragon Gaiden: Rise of the Dragons burns hot thanks to clever tag combat and a big roster of diverse characters, but it doesn’t burn very long as you hit some poorly executed platforming, uninspired roguelike elements, and a shallow end game.

title: Disney Illusion Island Review
snippet: If you’re after a frictionless, family-friendly platforming adventure that’s heavy on charm and light on challenge, then Disney Illusion Island fits the bill better than a muzzle on Donald Duck.

title: F1 Manager 2023 Review
snippet: F1 Manager 2023 is a textbook definition of what a yearly game should be. It's not just a good racing sim, it's one of the best management games on the market today.

title: BattleBit Remastered Early Access Review
snippet: BattleBit Remastered's early access version is a strong start to a nostalgic multiplayer shooter that values big battles over cutting-edge graphics.

title: Remnant 2 Review
snippet: Where Remnant: From The Ashes was a strong first draft, Remnant 2 is a revolutionary sequel and a sterling manifesto for the looter-shooter soulslike.

title: Exoprimal Review
snippet: Exoprimal's unique take on the hero shooter genre is a bold one – with its best modes and surprises hidden deep within its goofy sci-fi story – but a variety of fun exosuits, the simple appeal of tearing through thousands of dinos, and great multiplayer design make that grind an easy one to recommend sticking with.

title: Unholy Review
snippet: With the exception of the rich environmental detail to be found in its sinister underworld setting, Unholy otherwise offers precious little to praise. It’s not scary enough to succeed as a horror story, its controls are too clumsy to provide a satisfying stealth experience, and its enemy and puzzle variety are too limited to make any part of the journey feel truly distinct. What begins as an intriguing incursion into a cult-ruled realm soon unravels into a repetitive slog stuck in the shoes of an unlikeable lead character. Unholy is never quite unplayable, but it’s certainly uninspired, unwieldy, and unlikely to hold your interest all the way to its completion.

title: Pikmin 4 Review
snippet: Pikmin 4 adds variety to the series' traditional gameplay by offering options other than the grab-and-throw formula of the past, and brings an extra helping of top-tier levels after the credits roll.

title: Viewfinder Review
snippet: Spellbindingly surreal and stimulating to the end, Viewfinder is the freakiest form of photo mode in which every snap is a happy one.

title: Jagged Alliance 3 Review
snippet: Jagged Alliance 3 is a flexible, challenging, approachable throwback turn-based tactics game in which the only thing that feels truly dated is the humor.

game.name: Testament: The Order of High Human

title: Testament: The Order of High Human Review
snippet: Testament: The Order of High Human is a bland, frustrating, and buggy fantasy adventure that would have been a bad four-hour Skyrim knockoff. At 40 hours, it’s hard to bear.

title: Oxenfree 2: Lost Signals Review
snippet: Oxenfree 2: Lost Signals offers a highly personal and unpredictable horror-themed adventure that repeats a lot of the first game’s ideas, and it's still worth tuning into.

title: Everybody 1-2-Switch Review - IGN
snippet: Everybody 1-2-Switch is a paltry, unoriginal party game. Bringing it out is like inviting the fun police to bust up your shindig.

game.name: The Legend of Heroes: Trails into Reverie

title: The Legend of Heroes: Trails into Reverie Review - IGN
snippet: The Legend of Heroes: Trails into Reverie has a great story with an engaging turn-based battle system. While it does have issues with a bloated cast of characters, impeccable pacing makes every interaction manageable.

title: Crash Team Rumble Review - IGN
snippet: Crash Team Rumble does a great job of transforming Crash into a unique, flavorful multiplayer game with some incredible maps and fun characters, but a lack of modes, some balance problems, and an irritating unlock system means it’s more of a snack than a full meal.

title: AEW: Fight Forever Review
snippet: AEW: Fight Forever may have the audacity to challenge the current king of wrestling games, but despite its fun-loving spirit, strong sense of style, and solid fundamentals, it fails to execute on a level that comes anywhere close to its potential.

title: Park Beyond Review
snippet: Park Beyond brings a delightful sense of whimsy to park sims, but gets derailed by frequent bugs, poor staff control, and bad economics.

title: Trepang2 Review
snippet: Trepang 2 is a short but sweet tribute to classic slow-motion shooters like F.E.A.R. with great action and a throwaway story.

title: Dave The Diver Review
snippet: Dave The Diver is an aquatic adventure RPG that’s wholesome, wonderfully complex, and delightfully hard to put down.

title: Final Fantasy 16 Review
snippet: Featuring fast, reflex driven, action heavy combat, Final Fantasy 16 is certainly a departure from what fans may expect out of a Final Fantasy game, but its excellent story, characters, and world building are right up there with the best the series has to offer, and the innovative Active Time Lore feature should set a new standard for how lengthy, story-heavy games keep players invested in its world.

game.name: Story of Seasons: A Wonderful Life

title: Story of Seasons: A Wonderful Life Review
snippet: Story of Seasons: A Wonderful Life is an admirably updated remake, but those improvements can only do so much when the heart of it feels as dated as it does.

title: Aliens: Dark Descent Review
snippet: Aliens: Dark Descent is a fascinating, tense, inspired survival horror RTS with a few too many technical bugs to recommend it without caveats.

title: F1 23 Review
snippet: With Braking Point 2 aboard, F1 23 is a far heartier package than F1 22. However, while the new F1 World mode is packed with activities, its loot-based upgrade system may split fans.

title: Layers of Fear Review
snippet: Layers of Fear is an atmospheric tour through the shattered psyches of some seriously tortured artists, but predictable shock tactics fail to provide more than a handful of genuine scares, let alone layers of them.

title: Harmony: The Fall of Reverie Review
snippet: Harmony: The Fall of Reverie is a powerful visual novel that mixes a world-bending story with deeply personal stakes.

title: Greyhill Incident Review
snippet: Greyhill Incident is a tedious horror game that mangles everything from its story to its stealth.

title: Amnesia: The Bunker Review
snippet: Amnesia: The Bunker is a smaller, more self-contained episode in the groundbreaking horror franchise that shows Frictional still has some chilling, new tricks up their sleeves.

title: System Shock Review
snippet: System Shock deftly returns a classic immersive shooter to its place on top of the heap, updated for nearly anyone to enjoy.

title: Starship Troopers: Extermination Early Access Review
snippet: Starship Troopers: Extermination could become the premier bug-stomping romp for big teams, but in its current early state, mobile infantry can do the dying without you.

game.name: Hamlet or the Last Game without MMORPG Features, Shaders and Product Placement

title: Hamlet Review
snippet: This is a creative little point-and-click adventure. It's short, but I thoroughly enjoyed it as long as it lasted. The title is deceptive, as this game really has nothing to do with Hamlet. But if you like point-and-click games don't let that keep you from checking out this bizarre and clever little undertaking.

title: Street Fighter 6 Review
snippet: Street Fighter 6 is the most feature-rich a Street Fighter has ever been at launch, but even beyond that, it's roster of 18 characters is excellent, the new mechanics revitalize the one-on-one fighting formula, and it absolutely nails all of the little things that make for a stellar fighting game.
Diablo 4 is a stunning sequel with near perfect endgame and progression design that makes it absolutely excruciating to put down.

game.name: The Lord of the Rings: Gollum

title: The Lord of the Rings: Gollum Review
snippet: The Lord of the Rings: Gollum is filled with dull stealth, bad platforming, and a pointless story, and does little to justify why anyone should take the time to play it.

title: After Us Review
snippet: After Us has a wordless story that doesn't effectively communicate its environmentalist themes and platforming that frustrates with unreliable controls, but it does look fantastic.

title: Warhammer 40,000: Boltgun Review
snippet: Retro-shooting and fast-paced arenas suit the Warhammer 40K setting nicely, but Boltgun falls short of greatness because its ideas peter out too quickly. Some good level design and nice environments make up for a lack of variety.

title: Star Trek Resurgence Review
snippet: The story of Star Trek: Resurgence does an excellent job of capturing what makes Star Trek work by presenting strong characters and tough decisions. Some storylines lack closure and it doesn’t always run smoothly, but its heart is in the right place.

title: LEGO 2K Drive Review
snippet: LEGO 2K Drive is a wild and whimsical all-ages kart racer with an incredible custom vehicle creation tool that’s just about worth the price of admission alone.

title: The Outlast Trials Early Access Review
snippet: The Outlast Trials is a unique cooperative horror game with a lot of potential, but it's one of those early access games that's unpolished and lacking content.

title: Humanity Review
snippet: Humanity is a beautiful, modern reimagining of Lemmings that blends many different genres into its clever puzzles, and its powerful level editor has dizzying potential.

title: Darkest Dungeon 2 Review
snippet: Darkest Dungeon 2 takes a bold new direction for the turn-based tactics ideas established in the first game, but its early hours lean heavily on randomness and it can be a trial to stick with it until you see the light.

game.name: The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom

title: The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom Review
snippet: The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom is an unfathomable follow-up, expanding a world that already felt full beyond expectation and raising the bar ever higher into the clouds.

title: Honkai: Star Rail Review
snippet: With stellar storytelling and satisfying progression that offer dozens of hours of content that newcomers and hardcore players can enjoy, Honkai: Star Rail is a free-to-play RPG that might hook you for months – without making you feel like you have to pay.

game.name: The Last Case of Benedict Fox

title: The Last Case of Benedict Fox Review
snippet: The Last Case of Benedict Fox is a disappointingly uneven Metroidvania that’s just as likely to engage as it is to enrage.

title: Redfall Review in Progress
snippet: Redfall is a bafflingly bad time across the board. Plagued with bland missions, boneheaded enemies, and repeated technical problems, Redfall simply wasn’t ready for daylight in this state.

title: Stray Blade Review
snippet: Stray Blade tries and fails to free the Soulslike from gloomy settings and opaque storytelling, falling short of both its own ambitions and the genre’s standards.

title: Age of Wonders 4 Review
snippet: Age of Wonders 4 is a dizzyingly customizable fantasy 4X made mightier by a legacy that follows you across multiple campaigns.

title: Star Wars Jedi: Survivor Review
snippet: If Respawn makes a third game like Star Wars Jedi: Survivor and Fallen Order, it'll complete the best Star Wars trilogy in 30 years, hands down.

title: Tron: Identity Review
snippet: Despite its short length, Tron: Identity's visual novel/detective story is well worth playing – and replaying – to unravel the latest mystery of life on the Grid.

title: Disney Speedstorm Early Access Review
snippet: Disney Speedstorm is a fundamentally good kart racer currently buried beneath so much gacha garbage and so many currencies that it almost seems like a parody of the entire free-to-play genre.

title: Dredge Review
snippet: Dredge's slow dive into Lovecraftian horror intermingles with its simple fishing and sailing mechanics to create an approachable boat management sim that's as unsettling as it is relaxing.

title: Horizon Forbidden West: Burning Shores Review
snippet: A couple of poor boss battles aside, Burning Shores is a great new chapter with enough creative new bells and whistles to keep Horizon fans more than happy.

game.name: The Mageseeker: A League of Legends Story

title: The Mageseeker: A League of Legends Story Review
snippet: The Mageseeker: A League of Legends Story is fairly straightforward, but it nails the feeling of playing as Sylas. The story goes to some interesting places and is accompanied by some great bosses and a momentous finale.

title: Advance Wars 1+2: Re-Boot Camp Review
snippet: Advance Wars 1+2: Re-Boot Camp is a great return for this long-neglected series. While there isn’t much variety in the game modes, depth comes from the number of variables you can adjust to make every match feel unique.

title: Dead Island 2 Review
snippet: Dead Island 2 is a hilarious gore-fest and a competent zombie-slaying adventure, but lacks creativity outside of its great sense of humor.

title: Minecraft Legends Review
snippet: Minecraft Legends is an engaging strategy game that capably combines the established Minecraft world with a streamlined RTS structure.

title: Sherlock Holmes: The Awakened Review
snippet: Sherlock Holmes: The Awakened could have been an intriguing clash of Cluedo/Clue with Cthulu, but instead it’s just a case of squandered potential.

title: Crime Boss: Rockay City Review
snippet: Crime Boss: Rockay City is an overly ambitious air ball on all fronts, from its sloppy moment-to-moment gameplay to its largely abysmal voice acting – the worst of which sound like single takes spliced in with mistakes intact. There’s an earnestness with which Crime Boss has been put together that I do admire – as a kind of direct-to-VHS knockoff of Payday on a promising ’90s backdrop – and there is an inescapable novelty in seeing these de-aged Hollywood stars steering the story here. Unfortunately, the hokey charm on display is nowhere near strong enough to offset the repetitive and regularly frustrating mission design, its roguelike single-player rapidly becomes a total chore, and its co-op juice just isn’t worth the squeeze. Sadly, Crime Boss: Rockay City’s coked-up ego has been writing cheques its budget-priced body couldn’t cash.

title: Ravenbound Review
snippet: Ravenbound is a roguelike that starts out promising before being grounded by obtuse systems, frustrating loot, and lots of bugs.

title: Meet Your Maker Review
snippet: Meet Your Maker is a great start for a dungeon-delving shooter that’s as unique as it is hard to put down.

title: The Great War: Western Front Review
snippet: The Great War: Western Front is a deep RTS that shows a lot of attention to historical authenticity, though that can certainly bog things down.

title: Everspace 2 Review
snippet: Everspace 2 is the looter-shooter for space jockeys and wannabe pilots, packed with cool ship classes and action-driven arcade dogfights to keep up the pace.

title: Forza Horizon 5: Rally Adventure Review
snippet: Rally Adventure is more conventional than last year’s outlandish Hot Wheels expansion, but fusing the open-world foundations of Forza Horizon with the arcade accessibility of Sega Rally is still a winning combination.

title: MLB The Show 23 Review
snippet: MLB The Show 23 is a great step forward for the series, thanks in large part to its brilliant new Negro Leagues mode.

game.name: The Walking Dead: Saints & Sinners - Chapter 2: Retribution

title: The Walking Dead: Saints & Sinners – Chapter 2: Retribution PS VR2 Review
snippet: The Walking Dead: Saints & Sinners – Chapter 2: Retribution is still unacceptably janky on PlayStation VR2, but major improvements make it worth the hassle this time out.

title: Have a Nice Death Review
snippet: Have a Nice Death is a punishing but satisfying roguelike with some extremely tough boss fights, but it could be a bit less stingy with long-term progression.

title: Resident Evil 4 Review
snippet: The Resident Evil 4 remake is the series' most relentlessly exciting adventure rebuilt, refined, and realised to the full extent of its enormous potential.

title: WWE 2K23 Review
snippet: Though the Showcase mode isn’t as super as its subject, John Cena, the sharp focus on refinement instead of reinvention helps keep WWE 2K23 as the gold standard of wrestling sims.

title: Pizza Tower Review
snippet: Pizza Tower is a blisteringly fast 2D platformer with an unmistakable art style and unforgettable soundtrack that transcends its Wario Land inspirations and finds its own identity as one of the most energetic, fun, and creative 2D platformers in recent memory.

title: The Dark Pictures: Switchback VR Review
snippet: The Dark Pictures: Switchback VR is a fun arcade shooter, but its short length, a weak story, and lack of scares hold it back.

title: Destiny 2: Lightfall Review
snippet: A couple exciting additions like Strand don't stop Destiny 2: Lightfall from feeling like a disappointing step backwards.

game.name: Bayonetta Origins: Cereza and the Lost Demon

title: Bayonetta Origins: Cereza and the Lost Demon Review
snippet: Bayonetta Origins is a beautiful storybook brought to life as an imaginative action-adventure full of childlike wonder and endless charm.

title: Dead Cells: Return to Castlevania Review
snippet: Dead Cells: Return to Castlevania is a shining blueprint of how to do a crossover right, celebrating Castlevania's history while mixing it with Dead Cells’ own identity to make something that’s more than the sum of its already excellent parts.

title: Wo Long: Fallen Dynasty Review
snippet: Familiar issues with storytelling and loot aside, Wo Long: Fallen Dynasty is another great entry in Team Ninja's growing collection of action RPGs that features some of the best combat in the soulslike genre, even though those battles start to lose their tension due to a distinct lack of enemy variety to fill out the 20+ hour runtime.

title: Sons of the Forest Early Access Review
snippet: Even in early access, Sons of the Forest is already a strong survival crafter sequel that takes all the things The Forest did so well and does them a little bit better.

title: Gran Turismo 7 VR Review
snippet: Gran Turismo 7 is the same amazing simulation-focused racer elevated by VR in ways I wasn’t expecting.

title: Resident Evil Village VR Review
snippet: Resident Evil Village VR is a thrilling way to experience an already great game from a brand new perspective.

title: Company of Heroes 3 Review - Multiplayer
snippet: Outfoxing your human foes remains excellent in Relic's latest, Company of Heroes 3. The heart of this World War 2 RTS' combat and faction design is stronger than ever.

title: Demeo Review
snippet: Demeo is a delightfully-distilled tabletop RPG experience for up to four players that fits just as well in VR as it does on PC or PlayStation.

title: Scars Above Review
snippet: Scars Above is a journey into a mysterious planet that lacks polish, depth, and balance, preventing it from being a memorable third-person shooter.

title: Blood Bowl 3 Review
snippet: Blood Bowl 3 is a sloppy, muddy, buggy rehash of a better game.

game.name: Kirby's Return to Dream Land Deluxe

title: Kirby's Return to Dream Land Deluxe Review
snippet: Kirby’s Return to Dream Land Deluxe is a refreshed version of a fun but frustratingly uneven Kirby platformer that takes a little too long to reach its potential.

title: Atomic Heart Review
snippet: Atomic Heart is a highly imaginative, atompunk-inspired attempt at picking up where the likes of BioShock left off that makes missteps but definitely has the ticker to punch well above its weight.

title: Company of Heroes 3 Review - Single-Player Campaigns
snippet: Company of Heroes 3's single-player campaigns remix the series' signature WW2 combat, but they wrap it up in a boring, buggy strategic map and a mismatched story.

title: Metroid Prime Remastered Review
snippet: Metroid Prime Remastered honors a lauded classic and brings it up to code, fixing outdated controls and adding a slick coat of polygons and textures that make this masterpiece of a shooter shine once more.

game.name: Star Wars: Tales from the Galaxy's Edge

title: Star Wars: Tales from the Galaxy's Edge Enhanced Edition Review
snippet: Star Wars: Tales from the Galaxy’s Edge Enhanced Edition is an excellent way to showcase the best features of a fancy new PlayStation VR2. The first few hours of the story are its Episodes I and II (bad) but then things turn around and immerse us in Star Wars greatness.

title: Octopath Traveler 2 Review
snippet: Octopath Traveler 2 is a very enjoyable JRPG sequel that feels a little too safe and familiar.

title: Like a Dragon: Ishin! Review
snippet: Like a Dragon: Ishin! is an enjoyable and interesting spin-off from the Yakuza series, but not an essential one.

title: Wild Hearts Review
snippet: Wild Hearts’ building mechanics add a very fun twist to its familiar but well-executed co-op monster hunting formula.

title: Horizon Call of the Mountain Review
snippet: Horizon Call of the Mountain’s exciting highs elevate it just enough above its often monotonous climbing mechanics to create a fun first blockbuster entry into the PS VR 2 library.

title: Theatrhythm Final Bar Line Review
snippet: Theatrhythm Final Bar Line is full of charm and nostalgia, and its impressive 385-song tracklist makes it an enjoyable way to listen to and play along with the most memorable songs from the Final Fantasy series.

title: Deliver Us Mars Review
snippet: A rousing mystery brought to life by a great voice cast and nuanced character writing can't really save Deliver Us Mars from its shortcomings.

title: Wanted: Dead Review
snippet: Wanted: Dead's shallow combat, dated presentation, and poorly balanced difficulty are the tip of the iceberg of issues that ultimately sinks this disappointing action game from the makers of Ninja Gaiden.

title: Hogwarts Legacy Review
snippet: In almost every way, Hogwarts Legacy is the Harry Potter RPG I’ve always wanted to play.

title: Hi-Fi Rush Review
snippet: Hi-Fi Rush marches to the beat of its own drum with stunning animation, loveable characters, and stylish rhythmic action that are anything but one-note.

title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Cosmic Shake Review
snippet: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Cosmic Shake is a barebones platformer that manages to be supremely boring despite its SpongeBob-infused charm.

title: Dead Space Review
snippet: Dead Space is a superb remake and undoubtedly the definitive way to experience one of the best survival horror shooters that Capcom never made.

title: Forspoken Review
snippet: Forspoken’s flashy combat and parkour can be fun, but they aren’t enough to make its cliche story and barebones open world very interesting to explore.

title: Fire Emblem Engage Review
snippet: Fire Emblem Engage proves itself worthy enough to be counted alongside the legacy it honors so well.

title: One Piece Odyssey Review
snippet: One Piece Odyssey proves that a classic turn-based RPG is a good genre fit for the world and characters of One Piece, and that makes it rewarding despite a lack of tension in its all-too-easy fights and some frustrating quest design that pads out its length.

game.name: The Walking Dead: Saints & Sinners - Chapter 2: Retribution

title: The Walking Dead: Saints and Sinners Chapter II - Retribution Review
snippet: The Walking Dead: Saints & Sinners – Chapter 2: Retribution shifts the series' balance from survival horror to action with skill, but its story is weaker and its technical demands are beyond what the Meta Quest 2 can handle.

title: High on Life Review
snippet: High on Life is a laugh-out-loud funny space odyssey that’s chaotic, bizarre, and a ton of fun.

title: World of Warcraft: Dragonflight Review
snippet: Dragonflight is a sharply-written and breathtaking experience for the first few dozen hours, but suffers from somewhat threadbare endgame options.

title: River City Girls 2 Review
snippet: River City Girls 2 beefs up its decades-old beat-'em-up formula with RPG elements, witty humor, and dense combat options.

title: Dragon Quest Treasures Review
snippet: Dragon Quest Treasures is far from the richest jewel in the Dragon Quest crown, but a laid-back structure, charming world, and endless stream of rewards make it a relaxing RPG still worth admiring.

title: Need for Speed Unbound Review
snippet: Need for Speed Unbound hasn’t strayed very far from the fundamentals of 2019’s Heat, but its bold new animated style impresses.

title: Choo-Choo Charles Review
snippet: Choo-Choo Charles is a haphazardly assembled meme-come-to-life that’s short, silly, and exceedingly dull.

title: Call of Duty: Warzone 2.0 Review
snippet: Call of Duty Warzone 2.0's revamp is reinvigorating thanks to the social fun of proximity chat and the excellent new DMZ extraction mode.

game.name: Crisis Core: Final Fantasy VII Reunion

title: Crisis Core –Final Fantasy 7– Reunion Review
snippet: Even when its portable roots occasionally distract, Crisis Core –Final Fantasy 7– Reunion is the best way to play one of the PSP’s most beloved games, almost completely modernizing its graphics, combat, and music in the process.

title: Dwarf Fortress Review
snippet: A dedicated renewal for an already-legendary video game, the 2022 release of Dwarf Fortress brings it to a level where the casually interested can finally dive in and Strike the Earth!

title: Far Cry 6: Lost Between Worlds DLC Review
snippet: Far Cry 6: Lost Between Worlds’ interdimensional structure is an inventive way to cap off Dani’s story despite playing it safe in most other ways.

title: The Callisto Protocol Review
snippet: The Callisto Protocol is a satisfyingly gory spiritual successor to the Dead Space series, but it’s ultimately more of a striking modern mimic than a scary new mutation.

title: Marvel's Midnight Suns Review
snippet: Marvel's Midnight Suns is an expansive tactical RPG that makes great use of card game mechanics to inject variety and unpredictability into its excellent combat.

title: Pokémon Scarlet and Violet Review
snippet: The open-world gameplay of Pokémon Scarlet and Violet is a brilliant direction for the future of the franchise, but this promising shift is sabotaged by the numerous ways in which Scarlet and Violet feel deeply unfinished.

title: Warhammer 40,000: Darktide Review
snippet: Performance issues aside, Warhammer 40,000: Darktide's ferocious battles make for some of the most thrilling co-op action in years.

title: Gungrave G.O.R.E Review
snippet: Gungrave G.O.R.E oozes style and has a fantastic combat system, but a lack of variety, aggravating difficulty spikes, and often-frustrating design decisions keep it from reaching its full potential.

title: Evil West Review
snippet: Evil West is far from the most complex or innovative action game around, but it nails the most important parts of its old-school, monster-killing campaign.

title: Atari 50: The Anniversary Celebration Review
snippet: Atari 50: The Anniversary Celebration offers a completely new, very fun way to explore several decades of Atari arcade machines, consoles, handhelds, and PCs.

game.name: The Dark Pictures Anthology: The Devil in Me

title: The Dark Pictures Anthology: The Devil in Me Review
snippet: The Dark Pictures Anthology: The Devil in Me’s tour through a maniac’s mansion is disappointingly lacking in any real menace or surprise.

title: Goat Simulator 3 Review
snippet: Goat Simulator 3 is bigger, sillier, and packed with more low-stakes fun.

title: Somerville Review
snippet: Somerville has ties to modern legends Limbo and Inside, but it’s equally reminiscent of another Hall of Famer: Out of This World. The end result is a unique physics-based puzzle adventure that isn’t quite on the level of the games that inspired it, but is nevertheless an extraterrestrial nightmare worth exploring.

title: Pentiment Review
snippet: Pentiment is a clever Medieval detective story with a gorgeously-realized world that I can hardly recommend highly enough.

title: Among Us VR Review
snippet: Among Us VR is a strong remake of Innersloth’s social deduction hit. While Schell Games’ take could do with extra content, it makes up for this with polished gameplay.

title: Sonic Frontiers Review
snippet: Sonic Frontiers is an ambitious open-world adventure that mostly succeeds at mixing up the Sonic formula, even when some of its ideas fall flat.

title: Marvel Snap Review
snippet: Marvel Snap packs bold ideas, deep gameplay, a punchy presentation and lots of love for Marvel. Its approach to building a collection and randomness in gameplay won't be for everyone, but it's still well worth playing.

title: WRC Generations Review
snippet: With its absolute mountain of rally locations, WRC Generations is uncommonly generous package for gravel groupies, asphalt addicts, and mud-slinging maniacs alike.

title: God of War Ragnarok Review
snippet: An enthralling spectacle to behold and an even more exciting one to take the reins of, God of War Ragnarok melds action and adventure together to create a new, unforgettable Norse saga. Impeccable writing, pitch-perfect performances, knockout action – it’s a complete work of art from top to bottom.

title: Resident Evil Re:Verse Review
snippet: There are some good ideas in Resident Evil Re:Verse, but balance issues, a lack of content, and aggressive monetization make it little more than a sometimes-fun curiosity.

title: The Entropy Centre Review
snippet: The Entropy Centre is a more than competent Portal tribute with clever time-manipulation puzzles and an endearing cast, but it doesn't take its clever concept as far as it seems like it should.

game.name: Resident Evil Village: Shadows of Rose - Winters' Expansion

title: Resident Evil Village: The Winters' Expansion Review
snippet: When we look back at Resident Evil Village, The Winters’ Expansion probably won’t be considered an essential part of it for either story or gameplay reasons. Its story is short and the third-person mode takes out some of the scares. At least Mercenaries mode gets more Lady Dimitrescu!

game.name: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare II (2022)

title: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 2 Multiplayer Review
snippet:
Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 2's multiplayer moves the formula forward with bigger maps and more customization without sacrificing its bulletproof mechanics.

[OpenCritic note: IGN separately reviewed the multiplayer (8) and single-player campaign (6). Their scores have been averaged.]

title: The Valiant Review
snippet: The Valiant is a serviceable, squad-based RTS with a sturdy story, but it’s bogged down by backtrack-heavy battles and a bad final boss fight.

title: Mount & Blade 2: Bannerlord Review
snippet: A shallow grind of an overworld strategy game threatens to ruin enjoyment of Mount & Blade II: Bannerlord's unique and sublimely gritty tactical action.

title: Star Ocean: The Divine Force Review
snippet: Star Ocean: The Divine Force isn’t the best in the series, but a revamped battle system and a charming cast of characters at least make it feel like a return to form.

title: Victoria 3 Review
snippet: Victoria 3 is a remarkable, if a bit janky, nation-builder with ocean-deep political and economic systems that suck you in and don't let go.

title: Bayonetta 3 Review
snippet: One of the best combat systems in gaming gets even better with Bayonetta 3. It's story is a bit of a letdown and its wild action scenes take a toll on the performance in certain spots, but neither of those issues get in the way of Bayonetta 3 being a top shelf action game on the Switch.

title: Ghostbusters: Spirits Unleashed Review
snippet: Ghostbusters: Spirits Unleashed is an asymmetrical multiplayer game that’s mostly enjoyable, but badly needs more content.

title: Gotham Knights Review
snippet: Gotham Knights is a co-op-centric caped adventure that made my interest Wayne thanks to poor combat, a transparently predictable mystery, and grueling progression.

title: New Tales from the Borderlands Review
snippet: Gearbox's New Tales from the Borderlands successfully recaptures the charm and humor of Telltale's original adventure-game spinoff of the Borderlands first-person shooter series, but its attempts to stretch out the gameplay and the story don't fare as well.

title: NHL 23 Review
snippet: A long-overdue audio-visual upgrade finally lets NHL 23 take full advantage of the PS5 and Xbox Series X so that the revamped franchise mode can look as good as it plays.

title: Dragon Ball: The Breakers Review
snippet: Dragon Ball: The Breakers starts with a great idea, but doesn't live up to it, as loose controls, a bad camera, and live-service gacha mechanics more fitting of a free-to-play title remove a lot of the fun from the initial concept.

title: A Plague Tale: Requiem Review
snippet: A Plague Tale: Requiem is a safe but exciting sequel with an impressively alarming amount of rats.

title: The Last Hero of Nostalgaia Review
snippet: The Last Hero of Nostalgaia is a competent RPG with a world that helps it stand out from the pack, even if it doesn't stand too far above it.

Mario + Rabbids: Sparks of Hope is a major leap forward from atop the shoulders of its surprisingly smart and charming predecessor, bringing a wonderful amount of flexibility to its turn-based tactical battles.

title: Scorn Review
snippet: Scorn is a relentlessly unsettling delve into a surreal, macabre world of alien mystery, but the scariest thing about it is the dreadful combat.

title: PGA Tour 2K23 Review
snippet: There's not much to be excited about in PGA Tour 2K23, with poorly presented golfing on second-tier courses. It's fiercely accurate to the real sport but lacks personality and variety.

title: LEGO Bricktales Review
snippet: LEGO Bricktales can often get in its own way, but it’s still a fun adventure that breathes new life into what a LEGO game can look like.

title: Moonbreaker Early Access Review
snippet: Moonbreaker is a fun and approachable digital miniatures game with an emphasis on smart turn-by-turn tactics, but it’s tarnished by microtransactions that threaten its future.

title: Dakar Desert Rally Review
snippet: Dakar Desert Rally has a fabulous sense of scale and is capable of some great, unique racing, but it's undercut dramatically by frustrating handling, uneven performance, and some progression-killing bugs.

title: Slime Rancher 2 Early Access Review
snippet: Slime Rancher 2’s is a fantastic and stylish work-in-progress with strong bones, but little meat on them (at least right now).

title: Overwatch 2 Review
snippet: Overwatch 2’s switch to a 5v5 format breathes new life into what was once the sharpest shooter around. It just hasn’t quite recaptured all of that glory – yet.

title: Valkyrie Elysium Review
snippet: Respectable combat carries Valkyrie Elysium through a bland-looking action-JRPG with predictable story and straightforward level design.

title: Tinykin Review
snippet: Tinykin's vibrant levels and simple but satisfying platforming make collecting pollen a delight.
Obsidian has done a good job at all levels - universe, exploration, combat, construction, survival - for this beautiful and original survival title, a little more balancing and it will be perfect.
title: Grounded - Critique
FIFA 23 Legacy Edition (Switch) Review
snippet: FIFA 23 on Switch is a prime example of minimal effort for maximum profit.

title: FIFA 23 Review
snippet: FIFA 23’s slick and dramatic virtual football is fitting for the series’ last hurrah under its long-time name, but familiar frustrations abound, and it still greatly undervalues some of its most beloved modes.

title: Grounded Review
snippet: Grounded is a fantastic survival game with a killer premise, rewarding progression, and boundless creativity that even a host of bugs can’t spoil.

title: Games
snippet: An unapologetically tricky ode to street skating, Session is packed with a palpable love for the sport, but bugs and unsympathetic tutorial and mission design decisions undermine its approachability.

title: The DioField Chronicle Review
snippet: Real-time tactics meets old-timey politics in The DioField Chronicle, a fast-paced JRPG where quick and fun combat are paired with a generic story and some awkward controls.

title: Games
snippet: There are brilliant ideas in the ambitious early access release of Terra Invicta, and a lot of fun to find for strategy game enthusiasts, but you need to be willing to drill down into sometimes-opaque and confusing mechanics to do it. Politics are fun, if overwhelming, and the space layer is very cool, but the two just don't quite gel together yet. I do recommend Terra Invicta, but when playing takes so much trial-and-error to see results, my overall recommendation has to come with big caveats until it has spent some more time in early access.

title: Shovel Knight Dig Review
